content.inputFormat: Single line: JSON array of all arguments, e.g. [arg1, arg2, ...]

boilerPlates.Python3:
import sys, json
from typing import List

#  HELPER CLASSES  (do not modify)

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
#  YOUR SOLUTION  <- Write your code inside the method below
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
class Solution:
    def generateSentences(self, synonyms: Any, text: str) -> Any:
        pass

#  DRIVER  (do not modify)
if __name__ == '__main__':
    _args = json.loads(sys.stdin.readline())
    _synonyms = _args[0]
    _text = _args[1]
    _result = Solution().generateSentences(_synonyms, _text)

    print(json.dumps(_result))

solution.approach: The approach uses a union-find data structure to group synonymous words and then generates all possible sentences by replacing each word with its synonyms using cartesian product.
